external_narratives.summary: Detailed WartimeNI archive page for Lockheed Hudson T9328 crash at Slieveanee Mountain, Co. Antrim on 16 October 1940. All 5 crew from 224 Squadron based at RAF Aldergrove were killed. Three crew (F/L Francis Scott, P/O Ronald Davies, F/O Tony Tisdall) are buried at St Catherine's Church of Ireland, Killead-Aldergrove.
key_details: Three crew buried at St Catherine's Church, Killead-Aldergrove
F/L Francis Scott, P/O Ronald Davies from Dorset, F/O Tony Tisdall from Hendon

external_narratives.summary: Culture Northern Ireland feature on wartime air crashes including the Hudson T9328 crash at Slieveanee. Places the crash in the broader context of military aviation accidents across Northern Ireland during WWII.
key_details: Part of broader wartime aviation incidents in Northern Ireland
external_narratives.notes: URL dead as of 2026-04-06; URL dead as of 2026-04-06

external_narratives.summary: News coverage of the memorial plaque placed at the crash site above Cargan by the Glenravel Historical Society in 2015 to mark the 75th anniversary. The plaque was unveiled at the mountain crash site.
key_details: Crash site above Cargan, Co. Antrim
